content.text:
Two rectangles sit on the canvas: a styled one on the LEFT (the reference)
and a plain one on the RIGHT.

Restyle the RIGHT rectangle so its appearance exactly matches the LEFT
reference: same fill color, same fill pattern, same stroke color, same
stroke width, same stroke style, and same opacity.

Do not change the LEFT rectangle in any way, and do not move or resize
either rectangle.
Selecting a shape opens the style panel on the left — it controls fill,
stroke, and opacity.
Let me start by taking a screenshot to see the current state of the canvas.
I can see the canvas but I can only see one rectangle. The left reference rectangle might be off-screen to the left. Let me scroll or zoom out to see both rectangles.
Browser action failed: Keyboard.press: Unknown key: "ctrl"
Browser action failed: Keyboard.press: Unknown key: "shift"
Browser action failed: 'region'
Scrolled up by 5 at (700, 400)
I can see what appears to be part of a shape on the left edge. Let me zoom out using the minus button to see the full canvas.
Clicked at (34, 815)
Clicked at (34, 815)
Clicked at (34, 815)
Clicked at (34, 815)

Now I can see both rectangles at 50% zoom. The LEFT rectangle (partially visible on the left edge) has a red/pink diagonal striped pattern fill, and the RIGHT rectangle is plain white with a black border. I need to scroll left to see the full left rectangle. Let me scroll left to see it better.
Scrolled left by 5 at (700, 400)
The left rectangle is still partially cut off. Let me click on the left rectangle to select it and see its properties in the style panel.
Clicked at (225, 250)

I can see the left rectangle appears to be partially visible on the left side of the canvas. The style panel on the left seems to be showing its properties. I need to click directly on the left rectangle to select it. Let me try clicking on the visible part of the left rectangle (around coordinate 220-230, 250).

Let me first try to click on the left rectangle - it appears there's a shape just at the edge of the visible area with red diagonal stripes. Let me click more precisely on it:
Clicked at (222, 248)

I can see the style panel is already open, but I need to first click the LEFT rectangle to see its styles. The left rectangle appears to be mostly behind the style panel. Let me try clicking directly on the left rectangle. I can see its right edge around x=230. Let me first click on the left rectangle by clicking on its visible portion. 

Looking at the current state, the style panel shows:
- Stroke: cyan/teal color selected
- Background: light red/pink selected
- Fill: the first option (hatch/cross pattern) selected
- Stroke width: third option (thick) selected
- Stroke style: second option (dotted) selected

But let me first click on the left rectangle to confirm these are ITS properties. Let me try clicking right at the edge where I can see the striped rectangle.

Let me try using "Ctrl+A" to select all first to understand the layout, then click each rectangle individually:
Pressed key(s): Escape

I can see the style panel is still showing. The left rectangle is partially visible on the left edge. I notice the style panel shows the current state. Let me try to click the left rectangle by clicking on its visible part at around (222, 250). But first, let me try scrolling the canvas to center both rectangles properly. Let me use the page's read function to understand the page structure better:

I can see the page is an Excalidraw canvas. I can see the opacity is "60" in the panel (shown as "600" in the text). The style panel seems to be showing properties for the left rectangle that's selected. 

I notice the opacity label shows "600" which reads as "60" and "0". This is the opacity slider. The panel shows the properties of whatever is currently selected.

Let me try clicking directly on the left rectangle. Based on what I see, the left rectangle is mostly hidden behind the style panel on the left side. Let me click on the canvas where I can see the striped rectangle:
Clicked at (222, 250)

I can see the style panel is showing - it seems the left rectangle may already be selected (it has selection handles visible at ~220, 218 and 220, 282). The style panel shows:
- Stroke: cyan/teal (the 4th color - the highlighted blue one)
- Background: light red/pink (the 5th color in the background row)
- Fill: first option (hatch/cross pattern - the diagonal lines icon)
- Stroke width: 3rd option (thick, which appears selected/highlighted)
- Stroke style: 2nd option (dotted)

But I need to scroll down to see the opacity. Let me scroll down in the style panel to see the opacity setting. But first, let me confirm that these are the LEFT rectangle's properties by trying to click on the left rectangle more precisely. I can see the selection handles at the top-right and bottom-right corners of the left rectangle.

Looking at the style panel more carefully:
- **Stroke color**: The cyan/light blue one is selected (4th button in the stroke row)
- **Background**: The pink/light red is selected (5th in the background row)  
- **Fill**: The first icon (hatch pattern with diagonal lines) is selected
- **Stroke width**: The third (largest/thick) option is selected
- **Stroke style**: The second option (dotted dots ●●●) is selected

I need to scroll down to see opacity. Let me scroll down in the style panel area:
Scrolled down by 3 at (115, 350)

The style panel doesn't seem to scroll further - it appears the full panel is visible. The opacity must be above what's visible. Let me read the page text more carefully - I saw "Opacity600" in the page text, which means opacity is 60. Now let me understand the current state:

Looking at the style panel with a selected shape (the left rectangle appears selected based on the handles I can see at coordinates ~220, 218 and ~220, 282):

The LEFT rectangle's properties (from the style panel):
- **Stroke**: Cyan/teal (4th button in Stroke row - the light blue one)
- **Background**: Red/light red (5th in Background - the red color)
- **Fill**: Hatch pattern (first Fill button - diagonal lines)
- **Stroke width**: Thick (3rd button, which appears highlighted/selected)
- **Stroke style**: Dots (2nd button - ●●●, which appears highlighted)
- **Opacity**: 60 (from the page text "600")

Wait, I need to be more careful. The style panel always shows - let me first click on empty space to deselect, then click on the LEFT rectangle to confirm which shape is selected, then note properties, then click on the RIGHT rectangle and apply the same properties.

Let me click on an empty area to deselect:
Clicked at (700, 500)

The style panel is still showing even after clicking on empty space. I can see there are selection handles around the left rectangle (at ~220, 218 and ~220, 282). The left rectangle appears to still be selected. 

The style panel is always visible in Excalidraw and shows the properties of the selected element. Let me click on the left rectangle to select it definitively, then I'll read its properties, then click the right rectangle and update its properties.

Let me try clicking on the right edge of the left rectangle (visible at about x=225, y=250):

Actually, looking more carefully at the screenshot, I can see:
1. The left rectangle has selection handles at approximately (220, 218) - top right and (220, 283) - bottom right
2. The visible stripe pattern on the left rectangle confirms it's styled

The style panel is displaying the properties of the currently selected shape. Looking at the panel:
- **Stroke**: The 4th button (cyan/blue) appears highlighted with a darker border
- **Background**: The 5th button (red) appears highlighted
- **Fill**: The 1st option (hatch with diagonal lines) is selected
- **Stroke width**: The 3rd (thickest) appears selected
- **Stroke style**: The 2nd (●●●) appears selected

But I need to scroll the style panel to see the opacity. Let me check if there's an opacity slider below. Looking at the page source text earlier, it showed "Opacity600" which means opacity = 60.

Now I need to be sure the LEFT rectangle is selected. Let me look at the screenshot again - the left rectangle appears to have selection handles at roughly x=220. Let me try clicking right at x=220, y=250 to select the left rectangle.

But first let me try to understand - the style panel always shows, even without a shape selected. Let me click on the RIGHT rectangle first to see its default properties:
Clicked at (400, 253)

Excellent! Now I can see both rectangles clearly! The style panel has closed (I clicked on empty space apparently). I can now see:

**LEFT rectangle** (reference): 
- Has pink/red diagonal stripe fill pattern
- Has a blue dashed/dotted border
- The border appears to be a blue color with a dashed style

**RIGHT rectangle** (plain):
- Has white/transparent fill
- Has a plain black solid border

Now let me click on the LEFT rectangle to select it and see its exact properties:
Clicked at (185, 248)

The left rectangle is selected (I can see its selection handles at approximately 220, 218 and 220, 282). The style panel now shows its properties. I can see the style panel is partially cut off because the left rectangle is behind it. Let me carefully read the panel:

Looking at the style panel with the LEFT rectangle selected:
- **Stroke**: The 4th button (the cyan/teal colored one) is highlighted with a border - this is the stroke color
- **Background**: The 5th button (red/light coral) appears to have a border/selected state
- **Fill**: The 1st button (hatch pattern with diagonal lines) appears selected
- **Stroke width**: The 3rd button (thick line) appears selected (it has darker/highlighted appearance)
- **Stroke style**: The 2nd button (●●●) appears selected

I need to scroll down to see the opacity. Let me check what part of the panel might be cut off. The panel seems to stop at "Stroke style". Let me scroll within the panel area to see if there's more:

Actually, looking at the style panel, I see:
- Stroke colors: black, red, green, **cyan (selected)**, orange, blue
- Background colors: white/transparent, light pink, light green, light blue, **light red/pink (selected)**
- Fill: **hatch pattern (1st - selected)**, cross-hatch (2nd), solid (3rd)
